QUERY:
In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.

## Handle Time Policy
- Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname

4. products_orders

     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities

{"code": "import json
import pandas as pd

# Load data
casehistory_counts = locals()['var_functions.query_db:42']
cases_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:44']
casehistory_assignments = locals()['var_functions.query_db:40']

# Process data
counts_df = pd.DataFrame(casehistory_counts)
cases_df = pd.DataFrame(cases_data)
assignments_df = pd.DataFrame(casehistory_assignments)

# Identify transferred vs non-transferred cases
transferred_cases = set(counts_df[counts_df['assignment_count'] > 1]['caseid__c'].tolist())
non_transferred_cases = set(counts_df[counts_df['assignment_count'] == 1]['caseid__c'].tolist())

print('Transferred cases:', transferred_cases)
print('Non-transferred cases:', non_transferred_cases)

# Get case IDs in our date range
date_range_case_ids = set(cases_df['id'].tolist())
print('Date range cases:', len(date_range_case_ids))
print('Case IDs:', date_range_case_ids)

# Find intersection
non_transferred_in_range = non_transferred_cases & date_range_case_ids
print('Non-transferred in date range:', len(non_transferred_in_range))
print('Which ones:', non_transferred_in_range)

# Get owner for each non-transferred case
case_owner = {}
for _, row in assignments_df.iterrows():
    case_id = row['caseid__c']
    if case_id in non_transferred_in_range:
        case_owner[case_id] = row['newvalue__c']

print('Case owners:', case_owner)

# Count cases per owner
owner_counts = {}
for owner in case_owner.values():
    owner_counts[owner] = owner_counts.get(owner, 0) + 1

print('Owner case counts:', owner_counts)

# Filter agents with more than one case
agents_multiple = {owner: count for owner, count in owner_counts.items() if count > 1}
print('Agents with >1 case:', agents_multiple)

# Get handle times for these agents
agent_handle_times = {agent: [] for agent in agents_multiple}

cases_lookup = {row['id']: row for _, row in cases_df.iterrows()}

for case_id, owner in case_owner.items():
    if owner in agents_multiple and case_id in cases_lookup:
        case_data = cases_lookup[case_id]
        try:
            created = pd.to_datetime(case_data['createddate'])
            closed = pd.to_datetime(case_data['closeddate'])
            handle_time_hours = (closed - created).total_seconds() / 3600
            agent_handle_times[owner].append(handle_time_hours)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error processing {case_id}: {e}")

print('Handle times:', agent_handle_times)

# Calculate average handle times
avg_handle_times = {}
for agent, times in agent_handle_times.items():
    if times:
        avg = sum(times) / len(times)
        avg_handle_times[agent] = avg
        print(f"Agent {agent}: {avg:.2f} hours avg")

print('Average handle times:', avg_handle_times)

# Find agent with lowest average
if avg_handle_times:
    lowest_agent = min(avg_handle_times, key=avg_handle_times.get)
    print('Lowest agent:', lowest_agent)
    result = lowest_agent
else:
    result = None

print('__RESULT__:', json.dumps(result))"}

import pandas as pd

# Load data
casehistory_counts = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']
cases_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:54']
casehistory_assignments = locals()['var_functions.query_db:56']

# Process data
counts_df = pd.DataFrame(casehistory_counts)
cases_df = pd.DataFrame(cases_data)
assignments_df = pd.DataFrame(casehistory_assignments)

# Convert assignment_count to integer
counts_df['assignment_count'] = counts_df['assignment_count'].astype(int)

# Identify transferred vs non-transferred cases (using full dataset)
transferred_cases = set(counts_df[counts_df['assignment_count'] > 1]['caseid__c'].tolist())
non_transferred_cases = set(counts_df[counts_df['assignment_count'] == 1]['caseid__c'].tolist())

# Get case IDs in our date range
date_range_ids = set(cases_df['id'].tolist())
print(f'Cases in date range: {len(date_range_ids)}')

# Check intersection with non-transferred cases
non_transferred_in_range = non_transferred_cases & date_range_ids
print(f'Non-transferred cases in range: {len(non_transferred_in_range)}')

# Get owners for these cases from case history
case_owners = {}
for _, row in assignments_df.iterrows():
    case_id = row['caseid__c']
    if case_id in non_transferred_in_range:
        case_owners[case_id] = row['newvalue__c']

print(f'Found owners for {len(case_owners)} cases')

# Count cases per owner
owner_case_counts = {}
for owner in case_owners.values():
    owner_case_counts[owner] = owner_case_counts.get(owner, 0) + 1

# Filter agents with >1 case
agents_multiple = {owner: count for owner, count in owner_case_counts.items() if count > 1}
print(f'Agents with multiple non-transferred cases: {agents_multiple}')

# Calculate handle times for these agents
agent_times = {agent: [] for agent in agents_multiple}
case_lookup = {row['id']: row for _, row in cases_df.iterrows()}

for case_id, owner in case_owners.items():
    if owner in agents_multiple and case_id in case_lookup:
        case_data = case_lookup[case_id]
        try:
            created = pd.to_datetime(case_data['createddate'])
            closed = pd.to_datetime(case_data['closeddate'])
            hours = (closed - created).total_seconds() / 3600
            agent_times[owner].append(hours)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f'Error with case {case_id}: {e}')

# Calculate averages
avg_times = {}
for agent, times in agent_times.items():
    if times:
        avg = sum(times) / len(times)
        avg_times[agent] = avg
        print(f'Agent {agent}: {len(times)} cases, {avg:.2f}h avg')

# Find agent with lowest average
if avg_times:
    best_agent = min(avg_times, key=avg_times.get)
    result = best_agent
else:
    result = "No agents found"

print('__RESULT__:', json.dumps(result))"}

            # Clean case ID
            clean_id = case['id'].lstrip('#') if case['id'] else ''
            # Clean owner ID
            clean_owner = case['ownerid'].lstrip('#') if case['ownerid'] else ''

                'ownerid': clean_owner,
                'createddate': case['createddate'],
                'closeddate': case['closeddate']
            })
    except:
        continue

agent_avg_times = {}

for owner in agents_multiple.index:

        continue
        
    agent_cases = non_transferred_df[non_transferred_df['ownerid'] == owner]
    handle_times = []
    
    for _, case in agent_cases.iterrows():
        try:
            created = pd.to_datetime(case['createddate'])
            closed = pd.to_datetime(case['closeddate'])
            if closed > created:
                hours = (closed - created).total_seconds() / 3600
                handle_times.append(hours)
        except:

    if handle_times:
        avg_time = sum(handle_times) / len(handle_times)
        agent_avg_times[owner] = avg_time
        print(f'Agent {owner}: {len(handle_times)} cases, avg {avg_time:.2f} hours')

# Find agent with lowest average

    print(f'\nAgent with lowest AHT: {target_agent}')
